mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and stimulate corporate borrowing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across rolling 3-year windows when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, prompting short-term equity repricing upward as firms report margin expansion before supply constraints emerge.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during late-cycle phases yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 65 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation exceeding 0.4 percent monthly forces central bank tightening expectations that compress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL MoM growth surpasses 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate household income and corporate profit forecasts, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm broad-based demand strength that triggers immediate algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signals produce negative 5-day returns in 55 percent of overlapping observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that erodes corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 10-year samples.
